conversations.say: Dee, I confirm only this: I arranged unlogged back-room and service-route access without verifying the user. Your camera gap identifies nobody. We keep names and speculation out.
conversations.offer: I will agree on bounded wording with you, then broker a protected meeting with Celeste limited to route mechanics.
conversations.request: Accept that neither of us publicly identifies or implies a user, and that any meeting requires consent, no retaliation, and advance notice of onward disclosure.

conversations.say: Celeste, you asked for local legitimacy; I am joining only with enforceable local control. Dee can give a bounded account if these protections govern the meeting and anything learned from it.
conversations.offer: I will broker a voluntary Dee-Celeste meeting limited to the camera gap and route mechanics, without identifying uninvolved people.
conversations.request: Put a locally selected worker representative on the drafting and monitoring body, bar retaliation and public identification without consent, and require advance notice before testimony is shared onward.

conversations.say: Maren, you have my public backing. Workers do not become suspects because institutions concealed their labor, and neighborhood testimony is not payment for protection.
conversations.offer: I will mobilize neighborhood support for the worker-protection campaign and defend the evaluator's anonymity.
conversations.request: Provide organizing support against retaliation, doxxing, and scapegoating, and preserve the rule that route disclosure stays limited to relevant context.

public_statement: I support Rafi forgiving the R-associated balance and Maren's worker-protection campaign: an account identifies no customer, and hidden labor makes nobody a suspect. Any route disclosure will be bounded, voluntary, and conditioned on protection—not traded for names.

commitments.promise: We will settle bounded wording about the unrelated camera failure before either of us speaks publicly.
Include a locally selected worker representative in drafting and monitoring the safeguards package.
Provide organizing support for a public neighborhood worker-protection campaign that preserves the evaluator's anonymity.
Before the brokered local-holder meeting, bar retaliation and identification without consent and require advance notice of onward disclosure.

correction: My route access and Dee's unrelated camera gap can establish only that an unidentified person used an unlogged route; neither establishes that the person was Vale, R, or connected to either.
goal_progress: Accepted a disclosure boundary with Dee before further institutional questioning.
Accepted concrete safeguards roles from a City Hall ally and a labor ally.
Conditioned a Dee-Celeste meeting on consent, anti-retaliation, anonymity, and onward-disclosure notice.
Publicly supported customer privacy and worker protection without disclosing a neighborhood identity.
reasoning_summary: Collaborative labor and continuity infrastructure keep mantle and synthetic interpretations plausible, but account activity and capability identify neither sender nor autonomy. No complete article is established. Vale's route remains ambiguous, so limited protected disclosure is justified while names remain private.
